---
title: "Creating & Using Loops"
---

::: {.topic-badges}
[Advanced]{.level-badge .level-advanced} · **Technical & Agentic** track · ~35 min hands-on + readings and quiz
:::

[← Back to the Technical & Agentic track](technical-agentic.qmd){.track-back}

*Run the same step over a batch — the power and the risk of compounding errors.*

## What you'll be able to do

- Set up an iterative loop over many items
- Add checks so a small early error doesn't compound
- Decide when a loop should be supervised vs. fully manual

## Overview

A loop applies the same step across many items — summarize each of 50 documents, code each of 200 responses. It's where AI saves the most time and where errors scale fastest: one flawed step repeats everywhere.

The discipline is sampling and spot-checking: validate the loop on a few items, run it, then audit a random sample of the output.

## Practice activities

```{=html}
<details class="activity"><summary>Activity 1 · Advanced — Loop with a checkpoint</summary>
```

**Time** ~20 min · **Tools** ChatGPT Edu/Claude (+ R/Python if scripting)

**Goal.** Apply one task across many items, then audit the results.

**Setup.** About 10 non-sensitive items and one task. You can use 10 short [311 Service & Information Requests](https://opendataphilly.org/datasets/311-service-and-information-requests/) descriptions and classify each by issue type.

**Steps.**

1. Define the task for a single item and confirm it’s right.

2. Run the loop over all 10 (in the tool, or with a script that calls the model).

3. Audit a sample:

   > Audit a random sample of 3 of these outputs for errors and tell me what’s likely systematically wrong.

4. Fix the instruction and re-run only the failures.

**Expected result.** 10 processed items plus an audit of a random sample.

**Check your work.** A small early error repeats across the whole batch — sampling catches it.

**Common pitfalls.** Don’t trust a loop just because it ran. Higher stakes mean reviewing every item, not a sample.

**Stretch (optional).** Add a confidence flag so low-certainty items get human review.

```{=html}
</details>
```

```{=html}
<details class="activity"><summary>Activity 2 · Advanced — Decide the guardrail for scale</summary>
```

**Time** ~12 min · **Tools** ChatGPT Edu/Claude

**Goal.** Set the rule for when a loop needs full human review.

**Setup.** A real task you’d loop.

**Steps.**

1. Write the rule: review every item vs. a sample, tied to stakes and reversibility.

2. Pressure-test it:

   > Give me a scenario where sampling would be dangerous for this task.

3. Decide and document.

**Expected result.** A documented review rule for looped tasks.

**Check your work.** Would you defend this rule if one bad output reached a real decision?

**Common pitfalls.** Sampling is fine for low-stakes work; never for irreversible or rights-affecting outputs.

**Stretch (optional).** Add logging so you can reconstruct what the loop did (see Reproducible AI).

```{=html}
</details>
```


## Check your readiness

Answer these, then check — your score suggests whether to dive in or skim the readings first.

<div class="readiness-quiz">
<script type="application/json" class="quiz-data">
{"questions": [{"q": "The main risk of looping AI over many items is…", "options": ["A small error repeats at scale", "It's too slow", "It uses tabs", "It's boring"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Errors compound across the batch."}, {"q": "Before trusting a loop's output, you should…", "options": ["Audit a random sample", "Trust it if it ran", "Check the title", "Count the items"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Sampling catches systematic errors."}, {"q": "Higher-stakes loops need…", "options": ["More human review per item", "Less review", "No review", "A bigger model"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Oversight scales with stakes."}]}
</script>
</div>

## Recommended readings

Available in the shared OneDrive folder **Staff Faculty AI Workshop → Readings**, and online where linked:

- [**Loop Library: Repeatable AI Agent Workflows (Forward Future)**](https://signals.forwardfuture.ai/loop-library/) — patterns for reliable loops.
- [**The Coming Loop (Armin Ronacher)**](https://lucumr.pocoo.org/2026/6/23/the-coming-loop/) — where looped automation is heading.

## Useful resources

- [OpenAI: Building Agents](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/agents) — loop and workflow patterns.
- [ChatGPT Edu (Drexel AI Tools)](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools) — the approved tool, with data rules.

---

## Navigate

**Next in this track:** [Local Models via Ollama](local-models-ollama.qmd)

**Related topics:** [Building AI Agents with Natural Language](building-agents.qmd) · [Creating & Using Skills](creating-skills.qmd)

::: {.callout-warning}
## Data rules
Loop over **non-sensitive** items; audit a sample before acting on the results. See [Tools & Access](../tools.qmd) and Drexel's [AI Tools page](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools).
:::
